briefdescription.para: A structure to hold the Connector objects which in turn hold the connection information.

detaileddescription.para: Corresponds to a three dimensional structure: threads|synapses|connections

briefdescription.para: A structure to hold the node IDs of presynaptic neurons during postsynaptic connection creation, before the connection information has been transferred to the presynaptic side.

detaileddescription.para: Internally arranged in a 3d structure: threads|synapses|node IDs

briefdescription.para: A structure to hold "unpacked" spikes on the postsynaptic side if spike compression is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Internally arranged in a 3d structure: synapses|sources|spike data

briefdescription.para: Stores absolute position in receive buffer of secondary events.

detaileddescription.para: structure: threads|synapses|position

briefdescription.para: A structure to hold the information about targets for each neuron on the presynaptic side.

detaileddescription.para: Internally arranged in a 3d structure: threads|localnodes|targets

briefdescription.para: A structure to count the number of synapses of a specific type.

detaileddescription.para: Arranged in a 2d structure: threads|synapsetypes.

briefdescription.para: Dictionary for connection rules.
ConnBuilder factories, indexed by connruledict_ elements.
Value of the smallest delay in the network.
Value of the largest delay in the network in steps.
Whether to keep source table after connection setup is complete.
True if new connections have been created since startup or last call to simulate.
true if GetConnections has been called.
Whether to sort connections by source node ID.

Whether to use spike compression; if a neuron has targets on multiple threads of a process, this switch makes sure that only a single packet is sent to the process instead of one packet per target thread; requires sort_connections_by_source_ = true; for more details see the discussion and sketch in https://github.com/nest/nest-simulator/pull/1338.
Whether primary connections (spikes) exist.
Check for primary connections (spikes) on each thread.
Whether secondary connections (e.g., gap junctions) exist.
Check for secondary connections (e.g., gap junctions) on each thread.
Maximum distance between (double) spike times in STDP that is still considered 0.

detaileddescription.para: See issue #894

briefdescription.para: Prepare manager for operation.

detaileddescription.para: After this method has completed, the manager should be completely initialized and "ready for action".

simplesect.para: Initialization of any given manager may depend on other managers having been initialized before. KernelManager::initialize() is responsible for calling the initialization routines on the specific managers in correct order.

briefdescription.para: Take down manager after operation.

detaileddescription.para: After this method has completed, all dynamic data structures created by the manager shall be deallocated and containers emptied. Plain variables need not be reset.

simplesect.para: Finalization of any given manager may depend on other managers not having been finalized yet. KernelManager::finalize() is responsible for calling the initialization routines on the specific managers in correct order, i.e., the opposite order of initialize() calls.

briefdescription.para: Change the number of threads.

detaileddescription.para: Many data structures within the different managers depend on the number of threads. This function is called on each manager upon a change of that number and allows the manager to re-allocate data structures accordingly.

briefdescription.para: Add a connectivity rule, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: the respective ConnBuilderFactory.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &name, NodeCollectionPTR sources, NodeCollectionPTR targets, const DictionaryDatum &conn_spec, const std::vector< DictionaryDatum > &syn_specs)
memberdef.name: get_conn_builder

memberdef.argsstring: (NodeCollectionPTR sources, NodeCollectionPTR targets, const DictionaryDatum &conn_spec, const std::vector< DictionaryDatum > &syn_specs)
memberdef.name: connect

briefdescription.para: Create connections.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t snode_id, Node *target, size_t target_thread, const synindex syn_id, const DictionaryDatum &params, const double delay=numerics::nan, const double weight=numerics::nan)
memberdef.name: connect

briefdescription.para: Connect two nodes.

detaileddescription.para: The source node is defined by its global ID. The target node is defined by the node. The connection is established on the thread/process that owns the target node.

The parameters delay and weight have the default value numerics::nan. numerics::nan is a special value, which describes double values that are not a number. If delay or weight is omitted in a connect call, numerics::nan indicates this and weight/delay are set only, if they are valid.

parameterdescription.para: node ID of the sending Node.
Pointer to target Node.
Thread that hosts the target node.
The synapse model to use.
Parameter dictionary to configure the synapse.
Delay of the connection (in ms).
Weight of the connection.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t snode_id, const size_t target, const DictionaryDatum &params, const synindex syn_id)
memberdef.name: connect

briefdescription.para: Connect two nodes.

detaileddescription.para: The source and target nodes are defined by their global ID. The connection is established on the thread/process that owns the target node.

parameterdescription.para: node ID of the sending Node.
node ID of the target Node.
Parameter dictionary to configure the synapse.
The synapse model to use.

memberdef.argsstring: (long *sources, long *targets, double *weights, double *delays, std::vector< std::string > &p_keys, double *p_values, size_t n, std::string syn_model)
memberdef.name: connect_arrays

briefdescription.para: Connect nodes from SONATA specification.

detaileddescription.para: This function instantiates the SonataConnector class and calls the class member function connect.

parameterdescription.para: Specification dictionary, see PyNEST SonataNetwork._create_graph_specs for details.
Size of the hyperslab to read in one read operation, applies to all HDF5 datasets.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t snode_id, const size_t tnode_id)
memberdef.name: find_connection

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t snode_id, const size_t tnode_id)
memberdef.name: disconnect

briefdescription.para: Check whether a connection between the given source and target nodes can be established on the given thread with id tid.

simplesect.para: The type of connection as ConnectionType if the connection should be made, ConnectionType::NO_CONNECTION otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t source_node_id, const size_t target_node_id, const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid) const
memberdef.name: get_synapse_status

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t source_node_id, const size_t target_node_id, const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid, const DictionaryDatum &dict)
memberdef.name: set_synapse_status

briefdescription.para: Return connections between pairs of neurons.

detaileddescription.para: The params dictionary can have the following entries: 'source' a token array with node IDs of source neurons. 'target' a token array with node IDs of target neuron. If either of these does not exist, all neuron are used for the respective entry. 'synapse_model' name of the synapse model, or all synapse models are searched. 'synapse_label' label (long) of the synapse, or all synapses are searched. The function then iterates all entries in source and collects the connection IDs to all neurons in target.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::deque< ConnectionID > &connectome, NodeCollectionPTR source, NodeCollectionPTR target, synindex syn_id, long synapse_label) const
memberdef.name: get_connections

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of connections in the network.
Returns the number of connections of this synapse type.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< size_t > &targets, const size_t syn_id, std::vector< std::vector< size_t > > &sources)
memberdef.name: get_sources

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< size_t > &sources, const size_t syn_id, const std::string &post_synaptic_element, std::vector< std::vector< size_t > > &targets)
memberdef.name: get_targets

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t lid) const
memberdef.name: get_remote_targets_of_local_node

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid) const
memberdef.name: get_target_node_id

memberdef.argsstring: (const long vt_node_id, const std::vector< spikecounter > &dopa_spikes, const double t_trig)
memberdef.name: trigger_update_weight

briefdescription.para: Triggered by volume transmitter in update.

detaileddescription.para: Triggeres updates for all connectors of dopamine synapses that are registered with the volume transmitter with node_id vt_node_id.

briefdescription.para: Return minimal connection delay, which is precomputed by update_delay_extrema_().
Return maximal connection delay, which is precomputed by update_delay_extrema_().

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid, const std::vector< ConnectorModel * > &cm, Event &e)
memberdef.name: send

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t source_node_id, Event &e)
memberdef.name: send_to_devices

briefdescription.para: Send event e to all device targets of source source_node_id.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t source_node_id, SecondaryEvent &e)
memberdef.name: send_to_devices

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t ldid, Event &e)
memberdef.name: send_from_device

briefdescription.para: Send event e to all targets of source device ldid (local device id)
Send event e to all targets of node source on thread t.
Resize the structures for the Connector objects if necessary.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called after number of threads, min_delay, max_delay, and time representation have been changed in the scheduler. The TimeConverter is used to convert times from the old to the new representation. It is also forwarding the calibration request to all ConnectorModel objects.

briefdescription.para: Returns the delay checker for the current thread.
Removes processed entries from source table.
Clears all entries in source table.
Returns true if source table is kept after building network.
Returns true if source table was cleared.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t rank_start, const size_t rank_end, size_t &target_rank, TargetData &next_target_data)
memberdef.name: get_next_target_data

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t target_rank, const TargetData &target_data)
memberdef.name: add_target

briefdescription.para: Return sort_connections_by_source_, which indicates whether connections_ and source_table_ should be sorted according to source node ID.
Sorts connections in the presynaptic infrastructure by increasing source node ID.
Removes disabled connections (of structural plasticity)
Returns true if connection information needs to be communicated.

detaileddescription.para: False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets flag indicating whether connection information needs to be communicated to true.
Sets flag indicating whether connection information needs to be communicated to false.
Deletes TargetTable and resets processed flags of SourceTable.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called if connections are created after connections have been communicated previously. It basically restores the connection infrastructure to a state where all information only exists on the postsynaptic side.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid, const bool more_targets)
memberdef.name: set_source_has_more_targets

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const size_t lid, const synindex syn_id) const
memberdef.name: get_secondary_send_buffer_positions

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid) const
memberdef.name: get_secondary_recv_buffer_position

briefdescription.para: Returns read position in MPI receive buffer for secondary connections.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const bool called_from_wfr_update, std::vector< unsigned int > &recv_buffer)
memberdef.name: deliver_secondary_events

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid)
memberdef.name: get_source_node_id

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id) const
memberdef.name: get_num_connections_

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t tnode_id, std::vector< size_t > &sources)
memberdef.name: get_source_node_ids_

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, NodeCollectionPTR nodecollection, std::vector< size_t > &neuron_node_ids, std::vector< size_t > &device_node_ids) const
memberdef.name: split_to_neuron_device_vectors_

briefdescription.para: Splits a TokenArray of node IDs to two vectors containing node IDs of neurons and node IDs of devices.
Update delay extrema to current values.

detaileddescription.para: Static since it only operates in static variables. This allows it to be called from const-method get_status() as well.

briefdescription.para: This method queries and finds the minimum delay of all local connections.
This method queries and finds the maximum delay of all local connections.
Deletes all connections.

memberdef.argsstring: (Node &source, Node &target, const size_t s_node_id, const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const DictionaryDatum &params, const double delay=numerics::nan, const double weight=numerics::nan)
memberdef.name: connect_

briefdescription.para: connect_ is used to establish a connection between a sender and receiving node which both have proxies.

detaileddescription.para: The parameters delay and weight have the default value numerics::nan. numerics::nan is a special value, which describes double values that are not a number. If delay or weight is omitted in an connect call, numerics::nan indicates this and weight/delay are set only, if they are valid.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the sending Node.
A reference to the receiving Node.
The node ID of the sending Node.
The thread of the target node.
The synapse model to use.
The parameters for the connection.
The delay of the connection (optional).
The weight of the connection (optional).

memberdef.argsstring: (Node &source, Node &target, const size_t s_node_id, const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const DictionaryDatum &params, const double delay=NAN, const double weight=NAN)
memberdef.name: connect_to_device_

briefdescription.para: connect_to_device_ is used to establish a connection between a sender and receiving node if the sender has proxies, and the receiver does not.

detaileddescription.para: The parameters delay and weight have the default value NAN. NAN is a special value in cmath, which describes double values that are not a number. If delay or weight is omitted in an connect call, NAN indicates this and weight/delay are set only, if they are valid.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the sending Node.
A reference to the receiving Node.
The node ID of the sending Node.
The thread of the target node.
The synapse model to use.
The parameters for the connection.
The delay of the connection (optional).
The weight of the connection (optional).

memberdef.argsstring: (Node &source, Node &target, const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const DictionaryDatum &params, const double delay=NAN, const double weight=NAN)
memberdef.name: connect_from_device_

briefdescription.para: connect_from_device_ is used to establish a connection between a sender and receiving node if the sender does not have proxies.

detaileddescription.para: The parameters delay and weight have the default value NAN. NAN is a special value in cmath, which describes double values that are not a number. If delay or weight is omitted in an connect call, NAN indicates this and weight/delay are set only, if they are valid.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the sending Node.
A reference to the receiving Node.
The node ID of the sending Node.
The thread of the target node.
The synapse model to use.
The parameters for the connection.
The delay of the connection (optional).
The weight of the connection (optional).

briefdescription.para: Increases the connection count.
